operating_state: Revenue increased 2% to $6.94 billion driven by 14% volume growth across key products including Trulicity, Verzenio, Jardiance, and newly launched Mounjaro, offset by 7% realized price declines and 4% unfavorable foreign exchange impact. U.S. revenue grew while international revenue declined due to foreign exchange headwinds and China pricing pressures. COVID-19 antibody sales contributed $387 million as bebtelovimab transitioned to commercial availability.
cost_structure: Gross margin decreased to 77.3% from 78.9% primarily due to prior year COVID-19 antibody inventory impairment reversals, while nine-month gross margin improved to 76.1%. Research and development expenses increased 6% driven by higher late-stage development costs, partially offset by reduced COVID-19 antibody expenses. Marketing and administrative expenses rose 2% due to Mounjaro launch costs, offset by favorable foreign exchange rates.
material_events: Mounjaro received regulatory approval in the U.S., Europe, and Japan for type 2 diabetes treatment with strong commercial uptake following launch. The company recognized $206.5 million asset impairment charge for GBA1 Gene Therapy due to revised launch timing estimates. Acquired IPR&D and development milestone expenses totaled $62.4 million including PI3kα inhibitor obligations buy-out and Priority Review Voucher purchase.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange rates unfavorably impacted revenue by 4% from strengthening U.S. dollar against major currencies. The Inflation Reduction Act will enable government price setting for Medicare drugs starting in 2026, potentially affecting significant Lilly products and accelerating revenue erosion before patent expiry. Supply chain constraints and inflation pressures continue affecting manufacturing and logistics costs globally.
forward_outlook: Management expects tight Trulicity supplies to persist until additional manufacturing capacity becomes operational, with significant expansion planned for 2023. The company is investing over $3 billion in new manufacturing facilities to support incretin medicine demand. Tax law changes requiring R&D expense capitalization will increase 2022 cash tax payments by up to $1.5 billion.
uncertainty_profile: Bebtelovimab effectiveness against new COVID variants remains uncertain based on early data, potentially eliminating future demand for COVID-19 antibodies. Patent exclusivity losses for Alimta in major markets continue driving rapid revenue erosion with additional generic competition expected. Ongoing 340B program litigation and insulin access investigations could negatively impact business operations if resolved adversely.
